trivia_bullets.text: Fireshrieker appeared in Magic 2014, released July 19, 2013. Its original printing was in Mirrodin (October 2003, no. 171) — the set that introduced Equipment as a new artifact subtype with the keyword Equip. Fireshrieker was among the standout uncommons for its ability to double a creature's damage output. [Scryfall]

Double strike debuted in Legions (2003), with Ridgetop Raptor and Rockshard Elemental as the first creatures to carry the keyword. It was designed by Wayne Alward as a player submission for the 'You Make the Card' promotion and was rejected for that card before R&D used it independently. Fireshrieker combined the new Equipment mechanic (introduced in Mirrodin) with the new double-strike keyword in the same block era. [Draftsim: Double Strike in MTG; WebSearch]

Fireshrieker holds EDHREC rank 1,078, appearing in approximately 126,000 Commander decks (roughly 1.4% inclusion rate). Highest use is in Equipment-centric and combat-aggro Commander builds. Sword of Vengeance and Embercleave are frequently cited as higher-value alternatives, but Fireshrieker remains a budget double-strike enabler. [EDHREC via WebFetch; Scryfall edhrec_rank]

Christopher Moeller illustrated both the original Mirrodin printing and the M14 printing. Moeller has over 100 MTG card credits and is known for dark-fantasy, high-contrast compositions including Phage the Untouchable and Sword of Fire and Ice. The depicted weapon is a fantastical design — no real-world weapon archetype. [Scryfall]

The 'red-magic' tag was likely applied because the card art depicts a fire-wreathed weapon. Correct tag should be 'colorless-magic'.
